title: Branches, Integration, and Merge Evidence · Independent Repository Trial
summary: Integrate a short-lived change while preserving understandable topology and verifying both file content and combined behavior. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a branch-and-merge topology lab with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.
Switch branches only after predicting working-tree/index carryover, untracked collisions, and sparse or worktree constraints.
Find merge bases and explain three-way integration from two tips and their best common ancestor.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify merged tree, combined tests, log topology, diff range, and push target before publishing integration.
